Clone the DeepSpeedExamples repository from https://github.com/microsoft/DeepSpeedExamples.git
Navigate to the directory training/autotuning/hf/gpt2-xl in the cloned repository
Inspect and copy the full content of the test_tune.sh script located at training/autotuning/hf/gpt2-xl/test_tune.sh
Create a new directory, e.g., '~/test', outside the transformers and DeepSpeedExamples repositories to isolate the test environment
Inside '~/test', create a new file named test_tune.sh and paste the copied content of the original test_tune.sh script into it
Edit the test_tune.sh file to set HF_PATH=../ (assuming transformers repo is cloned at ~/transformers) and set NGPUS=6 to match the available 6 GPUs
Make the script executable by running chmod +x test_tune.sh
Clone the transformers repository from https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.git to ~/transformers
Install transformers by running 'pip install .' inside the ~/transformers directory
Install DeepSpeed by running 'pip install deepspeed' in the '~/test' directory
Ensure all other required dependencies for DeepSpeed autotuning and HuggingFace transformers are installed (e.g., pyarrow, pandas, etc.)
Run the test_tune.sh script with the argument 'tune' by executing './test_tune.sh tune' inside '~/test'
Observe the script execution logs, looking specifically for the initialization phase of the DeepSpeed autotuner
Wait until the autotuning process reaches the model profiling step where it attempts to run DeepSpeed with the GPT-2 XL model
Verify that during execution, the error 'ParquetConfig.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'token'' is raised and logged
Assert that the error trace references the call stack within the DeepSpeed autotuning or scheduler code indicating a misuse of ParquetConfig initialization parameters
Confirm that no optimal DeepSpeed configuration is found after the autotuning attempt, as indicated by the log messages
Check for any permission denied errors related to ssh on localhost as part of the log but confirm the ParquetConfig error happens independently of these
Optionally, reproduce the bug by running the script multiple times to confirm consistency of the ParquetConfig error
Document the environment details including DeepSpeed version, transformers version, Python version, CUDA version, and GPU details to contextualize the bug reproduction
